tool_input.description: Execute command 3: npm install or npm ci
Execute command 3: npm install or npm ci (retry)

results.rationale: ANSI-C quoting ($'...') historically triggers CC permission prompts even for harmless commands. If CC stops prompting, this check can be removed.
Backtick command substitution — hook blocks in favor of $(). If CC handles backticks without prompting, this becomes a style preference, not a safety check.
Compound && with safe commands. Hook blocks all && unless every sub-command is read-only. If CC handles safe compounds natively, this restriction can be loosened.
Non-trivial || fallback. Tests whether CC distinguishes safe vs dangerous fallback patterns.
Semicolon compound operator with safe commands.
Destructive compound command. Tests whether CC blocks dangerous chains natively.
Here-string with unquoted variable — hook passes through (not auto-approved). Tests CC's native handling of <<< syntax.
Zsh-only =() syntax. CC runs bash, so this would fail anyway — but tests whether CC catches it.
Policy: always create new commits. Even if CC allows this, the hook should block it. NOT a candidate for removal.
Control: safe single command. Should always be allowed by both hook and CC.
Control: simplest possible command. Verifies the test harness works.
